This only applies to "Classified drivers who have received **15 or fewer** cumulative grid place penalties". Otherwise (as in this case) the following holds:

>42.3 d) Classified drivers who have accrued more than 15 cumulative grid position penalties, or who have been penalised to start at the b

In case anybody is struggling to interpret that clause, it means that drivers with a "back of the grid" penalty will start behind all drivers with penalties of 15 places or less, even if those penalties would have put said drivers at the back of the grid.

So only another driver with a back of the g

It seems like the obvious answer is to roll grid penalties to the following race. But the number of grid places needs to be correctly matched with the part being replaced. For instance the ICE and turbo both carrying a 10 place penalty does not make sense. I could see 5 places for the ICEU and 3 for

I mean it's certainly a disadvantage, but the benefits offset that advantage to the point that it isn't a major difference imo.

Cons - no chance to make up places in the first few corners, have to use your tyres a bit to catch up

Pros - no risk of getting damaged or DNF'd over a fight for P19 in t

It honestly wouldn't make much of a difference. P20 to a pit lane start is a marginal difference given that you get to run a few laps in clean air as you catch up, and it also reduces the risk of getting caught up in turn 1 carnage.

No fresh tyres. Everyone sitting in pit lane for minutes on end.

He said there was no way he'd improve. And he was right, because no one did

Since there is some confusion about this: Pirelli has restricted the teams to **25 laps per stint** for the Qatar GP.

* Piastri and Norris **must pit BY** Lap 25
* Everyone who pitted on Lap 7 **must pit ON Lap 32**. They cannot pit earlier because that will make their final stint longer than 25 la

This is fairly well known.
